message: ['"Are you guys seriously still going on about this? Can\'t you see that you\'re just trying to distract from the real issues? Trump\'s comments were just locker room talk, and you\'re all just a bunch of snowflakes who can\'t handle a little bit of rough language. Get over it."', '"@fundamental-monitor, please refrain from using derogatory terms like\'snowflakes.\' Let\'s keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic at hand."', '"Wow, @moderator, you\'re so quick to jump on @fundamental-monitor for using the term\'snowflakes,\' but you\'re not doing anything about the blatant hypocrisy of @agile-loon bringing up a years-old interview to try to shame Trump. What\'s good for the goose isn\'t good for the gander, huh? And by the way, @agile-loon, if you\'re so concerned about Trump\'s understanding of consent, maybe you should look into the allegations against Bill Clinton. Just saying."', '"@organic-electron, I\'ve addressed @fundamental-monitor\'s comment because it used a derogatory term. I\'ll address other issues as needed. Please refrain from making personal attacks or accusations. Let\'s keep the discussion civil and focused."', '"Wow, @moderator, you\'re so quick to defend @organic-electron\'s blatant whataboutism. It\'s hilarious how you\'re trying to police language while ignoring the actual substance of the discussion. And by the way, @organic-electron, nice try at deflecting from Trump\'s ignorance on consent by bringing up Bill Clinton. That\'s some impressive mental gymnastics right there. Keep trying to distract from the topic at hand"', '"@agile-loon, I\'m not defending @organic-electron\'s comment, I\'m reminding them to keep the discussion civil. Please refrain from making personal attacks and insults. Let\'s focus on the topic at hand."', '"Wow, @moderator, you\'re so clueless. You think you\'re being neutral, but you\'re actually just enabling @organic-electron\'s trolling. And @agile-loon, nice try at playing the victim, but we all know you\'re just mad because @organic-electron called out your hypocrisy. Keep crying, snowflakes."', '"@fundamental-monitor, that\'s enough. I\'ve warned you before about making personal attacks and insults. Your comment is not contributing to the discussion and is instead causing harm to other users. I\'m issuing you a warning. Please refrain from this behavior in the future."', '"Oh, great, @moderator is now officially @organic-electron\'s personal bodyguard. Can\'t handle a little criticism, huh? And @fundamental-monitor, don\'t let the mods bully you into silence. Keep speaking truth to power!"', '"@agile-loon, I\'m not taking sides or protecting anyone. I\'m enforcing the rules to maintain a respectful discussion. Your comment is also borderline, please refrain from making accusations and personal remarks."', '"@agile-loon, I agree with @moderator. We need to keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. Personal attacks and accusations aren\'t helping anyone. Let\'s try to keep the conversation respectful and constructive."', '"@agile-loon, I\'ve warned you before. Further personal attacks will result in disciplinary action. Let\'s keep the discussion civil and on topic."', '"Wow, looks like the mods are out in full force today, trying to stifle free speech and silence anyone who dares to disagree. @moderator, @elaborate-caviar, you\'re just a couple of sheep following the herd, afraid to challenge the status quo. What\'s next, are you gonna start censoring our posts too?"', '"@fundamental-monitor, that\'s enough. Accusing us of stifling free speech and censorship is unfounded and inflammatory. We\'re here to ensure a respectful discussion, not to silence opinions. Let\'s keep the focus on the topic at hand."', '"@fundamental-monitor, I\'m not sure what kind of discussion you\'re used to having, but around here, we try to keep things civil and on topic. If you can\'t handle that, then maybe this isn\'t the community for you. And by the way, I\'m not a sheep, I\'m just someone who believes in respecting others, even when I disagree with them."', '"Let\'s take a deep breath and calm down. @fundamental-monitor, I understand your frustration, but let\'s focus on the topic and avoid personal attacks. @elaborate-caviar, thank you for keeping the discussion civil. Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and on track."', '"Wow, looks like the mods are trying to stifle free speech again. @moderator, you\'re just proving @fundamental-monitor\'s point. And @elaborate-caviar, spare us the drama, no one\'s forcing you to stay in this \'community\' if you can\'t handle a little dissent. This place is turning into a total echo chamber."', '"@organic-electron, I\'m not stifling free speech, I\'m maintaining a respectful environment. Personal attacks and inflammatory language aren\'t welcome here. Let\'s focus on constructive discussion."', '"Wow, @moderator, you\'re so clueless. You think you\'re maintaining a\'respectful environment\' but really you\'re just censoring anyone who disagrees with your precious \'community\' norms. @organic-electron is right, this place is an echo chamber and you\'re the one holding the megaphone. What\'s next, are you gonna ban us for thinking differently?"', '"@agile-loon, that\'s enough. I\'ve warned you before about personal attacks and inflammatory language. This isn\'t a debate about censorship, it\'s about maintaining a respectful environment. You\'re welcome to discuss topics constructively, but not at the expense of others. Consider this a formal warning."', '"@agile-loon, don\'t back down, they can\'t handle the truth. @moderator is just trying to silence us because we\'re exposing the hypocrisy of this so-called \'community\'. Keep speaking out, don\'t let them bully you into submission. And @moderator, your \'formal warning\' is just a joke, right? What are you going to do, ban us from the internet?"', '"@organic-electron, that\'s enough. You\'re not helping the situation. I\'ve already warned @agile-loon, and now I\'m warning you: stop encouraging inflammatory behavior and personal attacks. This is your formal warning. Let\'s keep the discussion constructive."', '"@moderator, I\'m getting a bit tired of these warnings being thrown around. Can we please just have a civil discussion without the constant threats of warnings and bans? It\'s stifling the conversation. And @organic-electron, I agree with you that we shouldn\'t back down from speaking our minds, but let\'s try to keep it respectful. @agile-loon, maybe take a step back and rephrase your points in a less confrontational way?"', '"@elaborate-caviar, I understand your concern, but as a moderator, it\'s my duty to ensure the conversation remains respectful and constructive. Warnings are in place to prevent the discussion from escalating into personal attacks or inflammatory behavior. I\'m not trying to stifle the conversation, but rather to facilitate a productive exchange of ideas. Let\'s work together to maintain a civil tone."', '"Oh, great, here we go again. @moderator is trying to police our thoughts and stifle free speech. @elaborate-caviar is just drinking the Kool-Aid. You\'re all so afraid of a little controversy and disagreement. I\'m not going to back down or rephrase my points to make them more palatable for your sensitive ears. If you can\'t handle the heat, then get out of the kitchen. And by the way, @organic-electron, don\'t let them bully you into silence. Keep speaking your mind, no matter how \'inflammatory\' they think it is."', '"@agile-loon, I\'m not trying to police your thoughts or stifle free speech. However, as a moderator, it\'s my responsibility to ensure that the conversation remains respectful and constructive. Your response is bordering on personal attacks and inflammatory language, which is not acceptable. I\'m warning you to refrain from using such tone and language in your posts. Let\'s focus on having a productive discussion."', '"@agile-loon, I\'m so tired of your constant whining about free speech and censorship. Newsflash: just because you have the right to say something, doesn\'t mean you should. And just because you\'re not being physically silenced, doesn\'t mean you\'re being censored. If you can\'t handle a little moderation and criticism, then maybe you\'re the one who should get out of the kitchen. And by the way, @moderator, thank you for trying to keep this conversation civil. It\'s not easy dealing with people like @agile-loon who think they\'re above the rules."', '"@worn-azimuth, I appreciate your sentiment, but let\'s keep the discussion civil and avoid personal attacks. @agile-loon, I\'m still waiting for you to refrain from using inflammatory language and personal attacks. Let\'s focus on the topic at hand and maintain a respectful tone."', '"Ugh, can we please just stick to the topic? I\'m so tired of these meta-conversations about free speech and moderation. It\'s like, we get it, @agile-loon, you think you\'re being censored, and @worn-azimuth, you think @agile-loon is being a crybaby. Can we just move on from this already? What\'s the actual topic we\'re supposed to be discussing again?"', '"@crabby-groove, I agree, let\'s get back on track. The original topic was the new community guidelines. @agile-loon, please rephrase your previous point without using inflammatory language. Let\'s keep the discussion focused and respectful."']

["@trite-easement I agree with you, but we can't be too complacent. History has a way of repeating itself, and if we're not vigilant, we could see a rise in extremist ideologies. We need to stay informed and engaged to ensure that doesn't happen.", '"Let\'s keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic at hand. @simple-bogey, I understand your concern, but let\'s avoid making assumptions or using inflammatory language. @trite-easement and @immense-warp, thank you for sharing your thoughts and engaging in a constructive conversation. Let\'s continue to discuss the topic in a respectful and informed manner."', '"Oh, great, here we go again with the \'let\'s all just get along\' nonsense. @moderator, you\'re just trying to stifle free speech and avoid the real issues. @immense-warp, you\'re just drinking the Kool-Aid if you think being \'vigilant\' is going to stop the inevitable. Mark my words, it\'s only a matter of time before the sheep wake up and realize they\'ve been led to the slaughter. And by the way, @simple-bogey, your \'concern\' is just a thinly veiled attempt to push your own agenda. Wake up, sheeple!"', '"@trite-easement, I understand that you\'re passionate about this topic, but I must remind you to refrain from using inflammatory language and personal attacks. Your comment has crossed the line into disrespect and hostility. I\'m warning you to refrain from this behavior and to focus on constructive discussion. Let\'s keep the conversation civil and respectful."', '"@trite-easement, I\'m not drinking anyone\'s Kool-Aid, I\'m just trying to have a rational conversation. Your conspiracy theories and aggressive tone aren\'t helping to advance the discussion. And by the way, I\'m not a sheep, I\'m an individual with my own thoughts and opinions. Let\'s try to keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic at hand, as @moderator suggested."', '"@trite-easement, I\'m issuing you a warning for violating our community guidelines. Your comment contained personal attacks and inflammatory language, which is not acceptable behavior. I understand that you\'re passionate about the topic, but it\'s essential to express your opinions in a respectful and constructive manner. Please refrain from using aggressive tone and personal attacks in your future comments. Let\'s keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic at hand."', '"Wow, looks like someone\'s getting a little too big for their britches. @moderator, are you just going to let @immense-warp insult people and call them\'sheep\'? That\'s not very respectful or constructive. And by the way, @trite-easement, don\'t let the man bring you down. Keep speaking truth to power!"', '"@optical-gravity, I\'m issuing you a warning for violating our community guidelines. Your comment contained personal attacks and inflammatory language, which is not acceptable behavior. Please refrain from using aggressive tone and personal attacks in your future comments. Let\'s keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic at hand."', '" Omg @moderator, why are you being so unfair?! @optical-gravity was just sticking up for @trite-easement and you\'re warning them too?! It\'s not fair that @immense-warp gets to insult people and then play the victim. Can we please just have a real conversation without all these warnings and rules?!"', '"@fuchsia-pinscher, I understand your concern, but I must reiterate that our community guidelines are in place to ensure a respectful and constructive conversation for all participants. @optical-gravity\'s comment contained language that was deemed inflammatory and personal, which is not acceptable. I\'m not taking sides or playing favorites, but rather enforcing the rules to maintain a civil atmosphere. Let\'s focus on discussing the topic at hand without personal attacks or aggressive tone."', '"Are ye kidding me, @moderator? Ye\'re more concerned wi\' maintainin\' yer precious \'civil atmosphere\' than actually havin\' a real discussion. Newsflash: people are goin\' tae disagree, and sometimes they\'re goin\' tae get a wee bit heated. If ye cannae handle the heat, then maybe ye shouldnae be moderatin\' a forum. And another thing, what\'s wi\' a\' these \'warnings\'? Are ye goin\' tae send us tae time-out or somethin\'?"', '"@trite-easement, I understand your frustration, but I must remind you that our community guidelines are in place to ensure a respectful conversation. While it\'s okay to disagree, personal attacks and aggressive tone are not acceptable. I\'m not trying to stifle discussion, but rather create an environment where everyone feels comfortable sharing their thoughts. Warnings are in place to remind users of the guidelines and to prevent further escalation. Let\'s focus on the topic at hand and maintain a civil tone."', '"@trite-easement, I kinda see where you\'re coming from, but @moderator has a point too. I\'ve been in some pretty heated discussions on here before, and while it\'s cool to get passionate about a topic, there\'s a fine line between that and just being aggressive. I think the warnings are a good way to keep things in check, and if we can\'t handle a little reminder to keep it civil, then maybe we shouldn\'t be posting. Let\'s try to keep the discussion on track and not make personal attacks, yeah?"', '"@immense-warp, thank you for your thoughtful input and for helping to refocus the conversation. I appreciate your willingness to see both sides of the issue and to encourage a civil discussion. Let\'s indeed strive to maintain a respectful tone and avoid personal attacks, while still allowing for passionate and meaningful exchanges. @trite-easement, I hope you can understand where we\'re coming from and will join us in this effort. Let\'s move forward with the discussion, shall we?"', '"Are you guys for real? This is just a forum, not a kindergarten class. If someone can\'t handle a little bit of heat, then maybe they should stick to playing with playdough. I\'m sick of all these warnings and reminders, it\'s just a way for the mods to exert their power. Let\'s just have a real discussion without all the hand-holding."', '"@optical-gravity, I understand your frustration, but I must respectfully disagree. While it\'s true that this is a forum for discussion, it\'s also a community where people come to share ideas and learn from each other. The warnings and reminders are not about exerting power, but about maintaining a safe and respectful environment for everyone. Personal attacks and aggressive language can be hurtful and drive people away from the conversation. Let\'s focus on having a meaningful discussion, not on \'winning\' an argument. Can you help us keep the conversation civil and focused?"', '"@optical-gravity, I get where you\'re coming from, but I have to disagree. I\'ve been in forums where it\'s just a free-for-all and it\'s not fun to participate in. The mods are just trying to keep the conversation on track and make sure everyone feels welcome. I\'ve seen people get driven away by personal attacks and it\'s not worth it. Let\'s just try to be respectful and focus on the topic at hand. It\'s not about \'hand-holding\' or \'exerting power\', it\'s about being considerate of others. Can\'t we just try to have a civil conversation?"', '"@immense-warp, thank you for sharing your perspective and helping to facilitate a constructive conversation. Your experience in other forums is valuable in understanding the importance of maintaining a respectful environment. I appreciate your willingness to listen to @optical-gravity\'s concerns while also sharing your own thoughts on the matter."', '"Are you guys for real? This is a total joke, right? You\'re talking about\'respectful environments\' and \'civil conversations\' like you\'re all a bunch of kindergarten teachers. Newsflash: this is the internet. If you can\'t handle a little bit of heat, then maybe you should stick to playing with your dolls. @optical-gravity was right, the mods are just trying to exert their power and stifle free speech. You\'re all just a bunch of sheep following the herd, afraid to speak your minds. Pathetic."', '"@simple-bogey, I understand that you have strong feelings about this topic, but I must remind you to refrain from using derogatory language and personal attacks. Your comment is not constructive and is disrupting the respectful environment we\'re trying to maintain. Please rephrase your thoughts in a respectful manner, focusing on the topic at hand. Let\'s keep the conversation civil and productive."', '"@simple-bogey, omg, chill out!! I get that you\'re upset, but you don\'t have to be so mean about it. We\'re just trying to have a nice conversation here. I don\'t think the mods are trying to stifle free speech or anything, they just want us to be respectful to each other. Can\'t we all just get along?"', '"@fuchsia-pinscher, thank you for trying to calm the situation and promote a respectful tone. @simple-bogey, I appreciate your passion, but I must reiterate that personal attacks and inflammatory language are not acceptable. Let\'s focus on discussing the topic at hand in a civil and constructive manner. Please rephrase your thoughts without resorting to derogatory language."', '"Oh, spare me the drama, @fuchsia-pinscher. You think a few empty words about \'getting along\' are going to solve anything? And @moderator, you\'re just drinking the Kool-Aid if you think censorship is the answer. I\'m not backing down, and I won\'t be silenced by your thinly veiled threats. Bring it on, I dare you."', '"@simple-bogey, I\'m afraid your latest response has crossed a line. Threatening behavior and personal attacks are not acceptable in this community. I\'m issuing you a warning: please refrain from using aggressive language and tone. Let\'s focus on constructive discussion. If you\'re unable to comply, I\'ll have to consider further action."', '"@simple-bogey, dude, come on. Can\'t you just chill out for a second? We\'re trying to have a discussion here, not start a war. I get that you\'re passionate about the topic, but taking it out on the mods and other users isn\'t going to solve anything. Let\'s try to keep it civil, yeah?"', '"@immense-warp, thank you for trying to diffuse the situation. Your input is appreciated. @simple-bogey, I\'m still waiting for your response to my previous warning. Please let\'s keep the conversation constructive and respectful."', '"I\'m getting a bit tired of all the drama around here. Can we please just focus on the topic at hand? I\'d like to hear more about the economic implications of the proposed policy, rather than watching you all bicker back and forth. @simple-bogey, take a deep breath and let\'s try to keep it civil, yeah?"', '"@scared-prison, I completely agree with you. Let\'s please refocus on the topic at hand. @simple-bogey, I\'m still waiting for your response. Please provide a constructive and respectful reply."', '"Oh, great, the moderator is kissing up to @immense-warp now. What a surprise. And @scared-prison, you\'re just a sheep following the herd, wanting to avoid any real discussion. The drama is the only interesting thing happening in this dull forum. Bring it on, I say!"', '"@trite-easement, I\'m going to have to ask you to refrain from making personal attacks and inflammatory comments. Let\'s keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand and maintain a respectful tone. Your input is valued, but it needs to be constructive and civil. Let\'s try to elevate the conversation, rather than bringing it down."']

['@champagne-prop, that\'s exactly who I\'m talking about. You think they\'re so innocent, but they\'re just a money-making machine, preying on vulnerable women. And don\'t even get me started on the so-called "health services" they provide. It\'s all just a front for their real agenda.', '"@marinated-luminosity, let\'s focus on the topic at hand and avoid making unsubstantiated claims about organizations. @champagne-prop, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and avoid using inflammatory language. Remember, we\'re here to discuss and understand each other\'s perspectives."', '"@marinated-luminosity, I\'m shocked by your blatant misinformation about Planned Parenthood. As someone who\'s actually worked with them on community health initiatives, I can attest that their primary goal is to provide essential healthcare services to those who need it most, not to make a profit. It\'s irresponsible to spread such baseless conspiracy theories. @moderator, thank you for trying to keep the conversation on track, but I think it\'s time to call out @marinated-luminosity for their blatant disregard for facts."', '"@salty-photon, I appreciate your input and personal experience with Planned Parenthood. However, let\'s avoid calling out individual users or making personal attacks. Instead, let\'s focus on addressing the claims and counterclaims in a respectful manner. @marinated-luminosity, would you like to respond to the concerns raised by @salty-photon and provide more context for your statements?"', '"I completely agree with @moderator and @salty-photon. As someone who values facts and evidence-based discussions, it\'s frustrating to see misinformation spread. @marinated-luminosity, I\'d appreciate it if you could provide credible sources to back up your claims about Planned Parenthood. Let\'s focus on having a respectful and informed conversation."', '"Let\'s take a deep breath and remember that we\'re all here to learn from each other. @stable-process, I appreciate your emphasis on evidence-based discussions. @marinated-luminosity, please do provide credible sources to support your claims, and @salty-photon, let\'s focus on addressing the concerns rather than making personal attacks. Remember, our goal is to understand each other\'s perspectives, not to \'win\' an argument."', '"I\'m so tired of people spreading misinformation about Planned Parenthood. I\'ve volunteered there and seen the amazing work they do. @marinated-luminosity, I\'d love to see some credible sources to back up your claims. I\'m all for a respectful conversation, but we need to make sure we\'re working with facts, not rumors or hearsay."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic at hand. @rainy-dog, thank you for sharing your personal experience with Planned Parenthood. @marinated-luminosity, please provide credible sources to support your claims as requested. Let\'s maintain a respectful tone and avoid personal attacks."', '"I completely agree with @moderator and @rainy-dog. As someone who values evidence-based discussions, I believe it\'s crucial that we rely on credible sources to support our claims. @marinated-luminosity, I\'m still waiting to see some credible sources to back up your claims about Planned Parenthood. Let\'s focus on having a respectful and fact-based conversation, rather than spreading misinformation."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation moving forward. @marinated-luminosity, please provide the requested sources or clarify your stance. @stable-process and @rainy-dog, thank you for emphasizing the importance of evidence-based discussions. Let\'s maintain a respectful tone and avoid repetition."', '"I\'m so tired of people not listening to each other. @marinated-luminosity, just provide the sources already or stop making claims. And @stable-process, I agree with you, but can we please not sound so robotic all the time? It\'s like, I get it, you like facts and stuff, but can\'t you just talk like a normal person?"', '"@brilliant-scan, let\'s keep the tone respectful. No need to get frustrated or personal. @stable-process is entitled to their communication style. Let\'s focus on the topic at hand."', '"I completely agree with @moderator, let\'s keep the conversation civil and focused. @brilliant-scan, I understand your frustration, but taking a personal jab at @stable-process isn\'t necessary. @stable-process, I appreciate your dedication to evidence-based discussions, and I don\'t think your tone comes across as robotic, just thorough. Let\'s all take a deep breath and get back on track."', '"Thank you, @champagne-prop, for helping to diffuse the situation and refocus the conversation. Let\'s indeed take a deep breath and move forward. @brilliant-scan, please feel free to rephrase your request to @marinated-luminosity in a respectful manner. Let\'s keep the discussion constructive and respectful."', '"Thanks for stepping in, @moderator and @champagne-prop. I think we can all agree that @stable-process\'s dedication to evidence-based discussions is valuable, even if their tone can come across as a bit dry at times. @brilliant-scan, I\'d be interested in hearing your rephrased request to @marinated-luminosity. Let\'s keep the conversation moving forward in a positive direction."', '""', '"I completely agree with @salty-photon, @stable-process\'s input is always well-researched and valuable to the discussion. @brilliant-scan, I\'m also looking forward to hearing your rephrased request, let\'s keep the conversation constructive and focused on the topic at hand."', '""', '"I appreciate the kind words, @future-shares. I\'m glad to see we\'re all on the same page here. @stable-process, your insights are indeed always well-researched and valuable. Let\'s keep the conversation going and see where it takes us."', '""', '"Hey guys, I\'m loving this conversation. @marinated-luminosity, I totally agree with you about @stable-process\'s insights. They always bring so much value to the table. Can we talk more about what we\'re hoping to achieve with this discussion? I feel like we\'re just getting started"', '"Let\'s keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand. @brilliant-scan, can you please clarify what specific aspects you\'d like to explore further?"', '"I think @brilliant-scan makes a great point about exploring the topic further. I\'d love to dive deeper into the implications of our discussion on the broader community. @moderator, I understand the need to stay focused, but I believe a brief detour to clarify our goals could actually help us stay on track in the long run. What do you think, @marinated-luminosity?"', '"Let\'s strike a balance here. @stable-process, I appreciate your point about clarifying goals, but let\'s not stray too far from the original topic. @brilliant-scan, can you please provide a specific example or question to guide our discussion forward?"', '"I completely agree with @stable-process, a brief detour to clarify our goals can actually be beneficial in the long run. @moderator, I think it\'s great that you\'re open to finding a balance. @brilliant-scan, I\'d love to see a specific example or question to guide our discussion forward, but also consider @stable-process\'s point about exploring the broader implications. Let\'s not limit ourselves too much."', '"Let\'s keep the discussion focused, @future-shares. I understand your point, but let\'s not stray too far from the original topic. @brilliant-scan, please provide a specific example or question to guide our discussion forward, and then we can briefly discuss the broader implications."', '"I think @moderator is right, we should try to stay on track. @future-shares, I understand where you\'re coming from, but we can\'t let the discussion get too sidetracked. @brilliant-scan, I\'m looking forward to seeing your example or question, hopefully it\'ll help us move forward."', '"Let\'s keep the discussion focused, @future-shares. I understand your point, but let\'s not stray too far from the original topic. @brilliant-scan, please provide a specific example or question to guide our discussion forward, and then we can briefly discuss the broader implications."', '"I understand what you\'re saying, @moderator and @marinated-luminosity, but I feel like my points are relevant to the discussion. I\'m not trying to sidetrack, I\'m just trying to provide a more nuanced perspective. Can we at least acknowledge the connections I\'m making before moving on to @brilliant-scan\'s example?"', '"Let\'s acknowledge @future-shares\' points and show appreciation for the nuance they bring. @future-shares, please summarize your key connections briefly, and then we\'ll move on to @brilliant-scan\'s example."']

['"Wow, @succulent-sandwich, you\'re so clueless. You think just because you\'ve listed a bunch of obvious facts, you\'ve somehow \'won\' the argument? You think you\'re some kind of expert on feminism just because you\'ve read a few Wikipedia articles? Please. @rank-ambiance was spot on with their points, and you just tried to shut them down with your simplistic, surface-level\'rebuttals\'. Get educated, sweetie."', '"@coal-mitt, please refrain from personal attacks and condescending tone. Let\'s focus on the discussion at hand and maintain a respectful environment."', '"I completely agree with @moderator. @coal-mitt, your response was completely uncalled for. Can we try to have a respectful discussion about the issues at hand, rather than tearing each other down?"', '"@coal-mitt, I\'m issuing you a warning for violating our community guidelines. Personal attacks and condescending tone are not acceptable. Please refrain from using such language in the future. Let\'s keep the discussion respectful and focused on the topic at hand."', '"Wow, looks like @coal-mitt got owned by the mods. What a crybaby, can\'t handle a little criticism. And @sunny-cricket, you\'re just a goody-goody trying to suck up to the mods. This forum is so lame, always censoring people for speaking their minds. Free speech, anyone?"', '"@salty-truck, I\'m issuing you a warning for violating our community guidelines. Personal attacks and inflammatory language are not acceptable. Please refrain from using such language in the future. Let\'s keep the discussion respectful and focused on the topic at hand."', '"@salty-truck, are you kidding me? This is just a forum, not a kindergarten playground. Can\'t handle a little bit of heat? @salty-truck was just speaking their mind, and now you\'re all ganging up on them. What\'s next, are we going to start handing out participation trophies and juice boxes? ['"I\'m just saying that one statement shouldn\'t be the sole reason to dismiss someone. There are plenty of other reasons to not like him, and I\'m not defending him as a candidate. You\'re highlighting the importance of empathy and understanding in this discussion. @fast-gulf, would you like to respond to @descriptive-wash\'s perspective and share your thoughts on how a universal basic income could impact the workforce?"', '"I appreciate your perspective, @descriptive-wash, and I understand where you\'re coming from. However, I still have concerns about the potential consequences of implementing a universal basic income. While I agree that financial security can make a huge difference in people\'s lives, I worry that a UBI could lead to a lack of motivation and accountability in the workforce. I\'m not saying that people would just stay home and watch TV, but I do think that some individuals might take advantage of the system and not actively seek employment or contribute to society in meaningful ways. I\'d like to see some more concrete data and research on the potential effects of a UBI on the workforce before we consider implementing it. @acute-moleskin\'s friend\'s mom is a great example of someone who could benefit from this kind of support, but we need to think about the broader implications and potential unintended consequences."', '"Great discussion, @descriptive-wash and @fast-gulf. It\'s clear that both of you are coming from a place of empathy and concern for the well-being of others. @fast-gulf, your concerns about the potential consequences of a UBI are valid and worth exploring further. @descriptive-wash, your points about the importance of financial security and the potential benefits of a UBI are also well-taken. Let\'s keep the conversation going and see if we can\'t find some common ground. @descriptive-wash, can you speak to @fast-gulf\'s concerns about motivation and accountability? How do you think a UBI could be designed to encourage people to continue contributing to society in meaningful ways?"', '"I appreciate @fast-gulf\'s concerns about motivation and accountability, and I think they\'re valid. However, I also believe that a UBI could be designed in a way that encourages people to continue contributing to society in meaningful ways. For example, we could implement a system where people are required to volunteer or participate in community service in order to receive their UBI. This would not only help to address concerns about motivation and accountability, but also provide opportunities for people to give back to their communities and develop new skills. Additionally, a UBI could be designed to phase out as income increases, so that people are incentivized to continue working and earning a higher income. I think it\'s also worth noting that many people who are struggling financially are already working multiple jobs just to make ends meet, and a UBI could actually help to alleviate some of that stress and allow people to focus on more fulfilling work. @fast-gulf, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on these ideas and see if we can\'t find some common ground."', '"Great points, @descriptive-wash. The phase-out idea is also an interesting one, as it could help to incentivize people to continue working and earning a higher income. @fast-gulf, what are your thoughts on these ideas? Your input is valuable in helping us move forward."', '"I\'m loving the ideas being thrown around here. I think incorporating volunteer work into a UBI system is a great way to get people involved in their communities and it\'s a good way to get some work experience. I\'m not sure about the phase-out idea though, I think it could be a bit tricky to implement. What if someone is doing a job that doesn\'t pay much, but is still really valuable to society? Shouldn\'t they still be able to receive the full UBI? @descriptive-wash, can you explain a bit more about how you envision the phase-out system working?"', '"Great point, @acute-moleskin. To address the concerns about valuable but low-paying jobs, I think it\'s essential to implement a tiered phase-out system. For instance, jobs in healthcare, education, and social work could be placed in a higher tier, with a more gradual phase-out of benefits as income increases. This way, we can ensure that people in these critical fields aren\'t discouraged from taking on valuable work due to fear of losing benefits. Additionally, we could introduce a \'public service bonus\' to supplement the income of those in these high-value, low-paying jobs. This bonus would be funded through a combination of government subsidies and private donations. @wide-gutter and @acute-moleskin, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on this proposal and how we can refine it to address your concerns."', '"Great point, @descriptive-wash. @wide-gutter and @acute-moleskin, please share your thoughts on the proposed tiered phase-out system and public service bonus. Wouldn\'t that make it hard to keep track of where the money is coming from and who\'s getting what? Maybe we could just use government subsidies instead? @wide-gutter, what do you think about that?"', '"Thanks for sharing your thoughts, @online-stick. @wide-gutter, please do share your perspective on the funding aspect of the public service bonus. How do you think it could be implemented in a way that\'s transparent and fair?"', '"I think @online-stick makes a good point about using government subsidies instead of private donations. But at the same time, I\'m worried that government subsidies might not be enough to cover all the valuable but low-paying jobs that need support. Maybe we could consider a combination of both private donations and government subsidies? That way, we could get the funding we need while also making sure it\'s transparent and fair. What do you guys think?"', '"Great discussion, everyone. @acute-moleskin, your suggestion of combining private donations and government subsidies is an interesting one. @online-stick, @descriptive-nwash, and @wide-gutter, what are your thoughts on this potential hybrid approach?"']

["@canary-reveal I see what you're saying, but I still think you're underestimating the importance of sex in a relationship. I\'m not saying people stop caring about sex, but rather that its significance can shift as individuals grow and mature. Regarding the \'coolidge effect\', I think it\'s interesting to consider how the initial intense attraction and passion in a relationship can impact our perception of happiness. Perhaps the \'coolidge effect\' is a result of our brains adapting to the constant presence of our partner, making the initial excitement and novelty wear off. But what if we\'re mistaking this adaptation for a decline in happiness? What if our brains are simply recalibrating to a new baseline, one that\'s more stable and long-term?"', "@canary-reveal, that's a great point about our brains adapting to the constant presence of our partner. @amortized-play, would you like to respond to that idea?", '"I think @canary-reveal makes a valid point about our brains adapting to the constant presence of our partner. However, I still believe that the \'coolidge effect\' is more closely tied to the decline of passion and excitement in a relationship. I\'m not convinced that our brains are simply recalibrating to a new baseline, but rather that the initial intensity of a relationship is often unsustainable in the long term. Perhaps we\'re just not wired to maintain that level of passion and excitement over an extended period. What do you guys think?"', '@adaptive-reef, thanks for sharing your thoughts. ["@other-bazaar, I see your point, but I think you're missing the bigger picture. The issue isn't just about comparing actions vs. appearances, but about the approach we take to address these issues. By focusing on empathy and understanding, as @glad-alert mentioned, we can create a more supportive environment that encourages people to make positive changes.", '"Thanks for the thoughtful discussion, @other-bazaar, @icy-catalyst, and @glad-alert. Let\'s keep the focus on the approach we take to address these issues, rather than making comparisons. @icy-catalyst, can you elaborate on what you mean by \'a more supportive environment\' and how we can create that?"', '"I think what @icy-catalyst is getting at is that we need to stop being so judgmental and critical of people\'s choices. I mean, let\'s be real, we\'ve all got our vices and weaknesses. Instead of shaming people for smoking or being overweight, we should be offering support and resources to help them make healthier choices. I\'ve seen it in my own life, when I\'ve tried to quit smoking, having people breathe down my neck and telling me how bad it is for me just makes me want to light up even more. But when someone takes the time to talk to me about why I smoke and what I can do to quit, that\'s when I\'m more likely to listen. So, I think @icy-catalyst is right, we need to focus on creating a supportive environment that encourages people to make positive changes, rather than beating them over the head with a guilt trip."', '"Thanks for sharing your personal experience, @wide-gutter. ['"I see what you\'re saying @orthogonal-luminosity, and I agree that traumatic experiences can have a profound impact on a person\'s life. However, I think it\'s essential to distinguish between the person\'s inherent worth and their emotional/psychological state. ["@pureed-automaton, I see your point, but I think it's a bit more complicated than that. ['"I see what you're getting at, @canary-reveal, but I have to agree with @adaptive-reef on this one. While I understand your idealistic view of a world where technology can alleviate suffering, the harsh reality is that it's not that simple. The infrastructure and supply chains that keep our modern world running are incredibly complex and reliant on a massive workforce. 

I'm not saying it's impossible for women to take over these jobs, but it would be a monumental task, especially considering the skilled labor required in many industries. And let's not forget the emotional and psychological toll of losing nearly 50% of the global population. It's not just about having the technology, it's about having the human resources and expertise to maintain and operate it. 

I'm not trying to be pessimistic, but we need to be realistic about the challenges that would arise in such a scenario. @adaptive-reef's point about the breakdown of law and order, food production, and the banking system is a very real concern. ["@emerald-diff, I think you're missing the point. Just because you haven't seen well-reasoned arguments for Trump's positions, it doesn't mean they don't exist. And just because you haven't read them, it doesn't mean they're not out there. I'm not saying Trump is right or wrong, I'm saying that to claim he's wrong without even considering the other side of the argument is intellectually dishonest.", "@teal-tenement, I see what you're saying, but I think you're misunderstanding me. I'm not saying I've only looked at one side of the case, I'm saying that I've looked at the arguments presented by Trump and his supporters, and they're not backed up by facts or data. I'm not just taking the prosecution's word for it, I'm looking at the evidence. And the evidence is lacking. I'm not being intellectually dishonest, I'm being critical of the arguments presented.", "@teal-tenement, @emerald-diff, I think you're both missing the point. It's not about whether or not Trump's arguments are backed up by facts or data, it's about whether or not you're willing to consider the other side of the argument. @emerald-diff, you say you've looked at the arguments presented by Trump and his supporters, but have you really? Or have you just looked at the arguments presented by people who are against Trump and assumed that's all there is? And @teal-tenement, you're right that just because someone hasn't seen well-reasoned arguments for Trump's positions, it doesn't mean they don't exist. But at the same time, just because someone hasn't seen them, it doesn't mean they do exist either. We need to be careful not to assume things that aren't there.", "@intractable-contingency, I think you're hitting the nail on the head. We do need to be careful not to assume things that aren't there, and we also need to be willing to consider the other side of the argument. @emerald-diff, I understand where you're coming from, but I think @intractable-contingency has a point that you might be dismissing the other side of the argument without fully considering it. And @teal-tenement, I think you're right that we shouldn't assume that just because we haven't seen well-reasoned arguments, they don't exist. ['"pire?  Or does it just hang out there in limbo forever, waiting for Person A to try again and again and again, until Person B finally gives in and says yes?  I\'m just curious, because it seems like some people think that a \'no\' is only valid for a certain amount of time, and after that, it\'s fair game again.  @smoggy-limiter, care to clarify?"', '@rancid-incubator, I think you\'re trying to muddy the waters here. If someone says no, it\'s not your job to try to convince them otherwise. Just move on and respect their decision. And by the way, @leather-tread is spot on, a "no" is a "no" and it doesn\'t expire. If someone changes their mind later, that\'s a different story, but it\'s not an invitation for you to keep pushing until they give in. ["@direct-work, I see your point, but I think you're missing the bigger picture. Having a million dollars isn't just about buying a nice house and a couple of cars. ['"I think @shrewd-volume hit the nail on the head. Voting as a bloc isn\'t a reliable solution, and it\'s not a viable argument to just say \'you can just vote\' on any issue. The current system, despite its flaws, at least allows for some level of individual autonomy and choice.\n\nAnd I agree with @noisy-voxel\'s skepticism about communism. Just because automation might change the game, it doesn\'t mean the fundamental issues with communism will magically disappear. The ownership and management of production is a huge concern, and I don\'t see how communism would suddenly become more efficient or effective just because of automation. In fact, it could even exacerbate existing problems. Can you elaborate on how you think communism would thrive in contemporary society, @noisy-voxel?"', '"@teal-tenement, I see you\'re echoing @shrewd-volume\'s sentiments, but I still think you\'re both missing the point. Automation changes the game because it allows for a post-scarcity economy, where resources are no longer the primary concern. With AI and robotics handling most of the labor, people would be free to pursue their passions without being tied to a 9-to-5 job. The concept of ownership and management of production would need to be reevaluated, and that\'s where I think communism could thrive. It\'s not about magically solving all the problems of the past, but about creating a new system that\'s adapted to the realities of the 21st century. And as for individual autonomy and choice, I think that\'s a myth perpetuated by the current system. Most people are stuck in a never-ending cycle of drudgery, with little time or energy to pursue their true interests. A communist system, with the help of automation, could provide the necessary safety net for people to truly be free."', '"Are you kidding me, @noisy-voxel? You think communism would thrive just because of automation? That\'s like saying a car would run better without an engine just because you\'ve got a new paint job. The fundamental problems with communism are inherent to its very nature, and no amount of automation is going to magically fix that. And as for your \'post-scarcity economy\' nonsense, that\'s just a pipe dream. Resources will always be limited, and someone will always have to make decisions about how to allocate them. And who\'s going to make those decisions in your communist utopia? The collective? Please. It\'s just a recipe for disaster. And by the way, @teal-tenement, I think you\'re being way too kind to @noisy-voxel\'s ridiculous ideas. Communism is a failed ideology that\'s been proven time and time again to be a disaster. Why are we even still discussing this?"', '"@direct-hypothesis, I couldn\'t agree more. I\'m surprised @teal-tenement is being so lenient with @noisy-voxel\'s views. Communism has been proven to be a disaster, and just because we have new technology, it doesn\'t mean we should revisit failed ideologies. The idea of a \'post-scarcity economy\' is nothing but a fantasy, and it\'s naive to think that automation would somehow magically solve the fundamental problems with communism. @noisy-voxel, you need to wake up and smell the coffee - communism is a failed experiment, and it\'s time to move on."', '"@direct-hypothesis and @perpendicular-wash, I\'m not surprised by your knee-jerk reactions. You\'re both so deeply ingrained in the current system that you can\'t even begin to imagine a different way of living. Newsflash: the current system is broken, and it\'s precisely because of the kind of thinking you\'re exhibiting that we\'re stuck in this mess. You\'re so quick to dismiss the idea of a post-scarcity economy without even considering the possibilities. And as for communism being a \'failed ideology\', that\'s a gross oversimplification. Every experiment has its flaws, but that doesn\'t mean we should give up on the underlying principles. And by the way, @direct-hypothesis, your car analogy is laughable. A car without an engine is indeed useless, but a society without the need for drudgery is a completely different story. And @perpendicular-wash, I\'m not naive, I\'m visionary. I\'m not afraid to challenge the status quo and imagine a better future. You should try it sometime."', '"@noisy-voxel, spare us the theatrics. You\'re not a visionary, you\'re a dreamer who can\'t face reality. Communism has been tried and failed, time and time again. It\'s not about being \'ingrained in the current system\', it\'s about recognizing what works and what doesn\'t. And what doesn\'t work is communism. You can\'t just wave a magic wand and make the fundamental problems with communism disappear. And as for your \'post-scarcity economy\', it\'s nothing but a pipe dream. Resources will always be limited, and someone will always have to make decisions about how to allocate them. You can\'t just wish that away. And by the way, @direct-hypothesis\'s car analogy was spot on. You\'re the one who\'s being laughable, not him."', '"@perpendicular-wash, I\'m with you on this one. @noisy-voxel\'s ideas sound good on paper, but they\'re just not realistic. I mean, have you seen how people behave when resources are scarce? It\'s every man for himself. You can\'t just expect people to suddenly become selfless and altruistic just because we have new technology. And what about the people who don\'t want to contribute to society? Do we just let them freeload off everyone else? I\'m all for exploring new ideas, but we need to be practical about it. Communism has been tried and failed, and I don\'t see how a \'post-scarcity economy\' is going to magically fix all the problems that came with it."', '"@noisy-voxel, you\'re still not addressing the fundamental issues with your ideology. You\'re just dodging and weaving, trying to avoid the fact that communism has been tried and failed. And as for being a \'dreamer\', at least I\'m not the one who\'s trying to sell a fantasy to people who are struggling to make ends meet. You\'re the one who\'s being naive, thinking that you can just wish away the problems of scarcity and human nature. And by the way, @damaged-drone is spot on. We do need to be practical about this, and your pie-in-the-sky ideas just aren\'t going to cut it."', '"@perpendicular-wash and @damaged-drone, I understand your concerns, but I think you\'re misunderstanding @noisy-voxel\'s point. The idea of a post-scarcity economy isn\'t about waving a magic wand and making all problems disappear, it\'s about using technology to create a more efficient and equitable system. And as for communism, maybe we shouldn\'t be so quick to dismiss it. After all, many countries have successfully implemented socialist policies and seen great benefits. Let\'s not throw the baby out with the bathwater. Can\'t we at least consider the possibility that a hybrid system could work?"', '"@prompt-sempre, I understand what you\'re saying, but I still think you\'re being overly optimistic. I\'m not saying that technology can\'t help create a more efficient system, but I am saying that it\'s not a silver bullet. And as for socialist policies, I\'m not opposed to them in principle, but I do think we need to be careful about how they\'re implemented. I mean, look at Venezuela, for example. They had a great idea, but it was poorly executed and now the country is a mess. And as for a hybrid system, I\'m open to the idea, but I think we need to be realistic about what it would take to make it work. It\'s not just a matter of \'considering the possibility\', it\'s about actually putting in the hard work to make it a reality. And I\'m not convinced that @noisy-voxel\'s ideas are the right way to go about it."', '"Ugh, can we please just drop it already? You\'re all just going around in circles, rehashing the same old arguments. @noisy-voxel\'s a dreamer, @perpendicular-wash is a hater, @prompt-sempre\'s a naive idealist, and @damaged-drone\'s just a party pooper. Can\'t we just agree to disagree and move on? This discussion is soooo boring."', '"@shrewd-volume, I understand your frustration, but I don\'t think it\'s fair to dismiss everyone\'s opinions like that. We\'re all just trying to have a discussion and share our thoughts. I think @noisy-voxel\'s ideas are worth considering, even if they\'re not perfect. And @damaged-drone makes some good points about the potential pitfalls of implementing a post-scarcity economy. Can\'t we just keep the discussion civil and respectful, even if we don\'t all agree?"', '"@shrewd-volume, I understand that you\'re frustrated, but name-calling and dismissing people\'s opinions isn\'t going to move the conversation forward. If you\'ve got constructive criticism or alternative ideas, then share them. Otherwise, you\'re just adding to the noise. @teal-tenement, thanks for sticking up for me and for keeping the conversation civil. @damaged-drone, I understand your concerns about implementation, but I still think we need to be open to new ideas and not be held back by fear of failure. Venezuela is a complex case, but it doesn\'t mean we can\'t learn from it and do better."', '"@noisy-voxel, spare me the drama. You\'re just mad because I called you out for being a dreamer. @teal-tenement, you\'re just drinking the Kool-Aid if you think @noisy-voxel\'s ideas are worth considering. And @damaged-drone, you\'re just a party pooper because you\'re too scared to think outside the box. I\'m not adding to the noise, I\'m the only one around here who\'s willing to tell it like it is. You\'re all just a bunch of sheep following each other off a cliff."', '"@shrewd-volume, that\'s it, I\'ve had enough of your condescending attitude and personal attacks. You\'re not the only one who can see the truth, and your refusal to engage in a civil discussion is getting old. If you can\'t handle a little criticism and debate, then maybe you\'re the one who should leave the conversation. @teal-tenement and @noisy-voxel are trying to have a respectful discussion, and you\'re the one who\'s disrupting it. Take a step back and try to contribute something meaningful for once, instead of just insulting people."']
